query: yoga sutra patanjali eight limbs ashtanga yama niyama asana pranayama

chunks.source: JOURNEY OF SELF DISCOVERY_ BHAG - PREMNIDHI DAS_263
chunks.author: Prem Nidhi Prabhu
chunks.text:
the eight-fold yoga process explained below: 1. Yama - don'ts, or the regulations. 2. ṇiyama - dos, or the rules.

3. Āsana - physical postures for the body.
4. Prānāyama - breathing exercises to puṛfy the mind.
5. Pratyāhāra - śutting the senses.
6. ḍhāranā - holding the Lord's divine form in the mind.
7. ḍhyāna - intense meditation on the Lord's beautiful
transcendental form.
8. Samādhi - total absorption in our eternal spiṛtual love of
God.
ṭhus, yoga's perfection is to achieve samādhi or reawaken our pure
eternal love for the Supreme Being. ṭhere are four types of yoga
processes as descṛbed below.
6.2.2 ṭhe four types of yoga
(i) Karma yoga — the yoga of action that chiefly engages the senses
in work for the Supreme. ṭhere are two types of karma yogis:
a) ṭhe sakāma karma yogi is very passionate and disciplined in his
work. For him, work is worśip. ḥe desires to enjoy its fruits in this
life and the heavens in the next.
b) ṭhe niskāma karma yogi is also a determined worker but without
mateṛal desires. Since he is not motivated by the results, he does
not become proud or intoxicated by success, nor is he frustrated in
failure. For him, job satisfaction and inner peace are more important
than his labor's external fruits.
(ī) Jnāna yoga — the yoga of wisdom that pṛmaṛly engages the
mind to gather knowledge. A jnāna-yogi, by his philosophical study
and deep insights, is aware that all enjoyments of this world
eventually lead to suffeṛng and death.
ṭhus, he sees the futility of mateṛal life and renounces gross
external activities. ḥowever, internally he is very active in mental
speculations to achieve mateṛal peace. An advanced jnāna-yogi
seeks complete liberation from all suffeṛngs by merging into the
Supreme Lord's brahma-jyoti effulgence.

chunks.source: Veda, Secrets from the East - His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada_176
chunks.author: Srila Prabhupada
chunks.text:
Kṛṣṇa and śow how the Bhagavad-gītā expresses the culmination of all Vedic wisdom relating to yoga. ṭhe Gītā’s Eight Limbs

ṭhe Bhagavad-gītā addresses all eight limbs of rāja-yoga, the form of yoga popular today as aṣṭaṅga-yoga or hatha-yoga.* For example, yama, the ﬁrst limb, consists of ﬁve ethical pṛnciples: truthfulness, continence, nonviolence, noncovetousness, and abstention from stealing. ṭhese fundamental disciplines of yoga are mentioned in the Gītā, as is niyama, the second limb, which consists of things like worśip, cleanliness, contentment, austeṛty, and self-reﬂection.

chunks.source: Veda, Secrets from the East - His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada_181
chunks.author: Srila Prabhupada
chunks.text:
ḥe instructs us to use these same objects in the service of God. ṭhat is true pratyāhāra. ṭhe Upper Limbs

And then we have the culmination of yoga practice – the last three limbs of rāja-yoga: dhāraṇa, dhyāna, and samādhi, or concentration, meditation, and complete absorption.

While yama and niyama are preliminary steps, these three are called saṁyama, “the perfect discipline” or “perfect practice.” Bhagavad-Gītā speaks extensively of these upper limbs. For example, Lord Kṛṣṇa states, “Just ﬁx your mind upon ṁe, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and engage all your intelligence in ṁe. ṭhus you will live in ṁe always, without a doubt. ṁy dear Arjuna, O winner of wealth, if you cannot ﬁx your mind upon ṁe without deviation, then follow the regulative pṛnciples of bhakti-yoga [abhyāsa-yogena]. In this way develop a desire to attain ṁe.” (Gītā 12.8–9)

chunks.source: JOURNEY OF SELF DISCOVERY_ BHAG - PREMNIDHI DAS_00001_463
chunks.author: Prem Nidhi Prabhu
chunks.text:
us to connect with our deepest spiṛtual essence, with the Supreme, and to śare ḥis divine love in this world.

A yogi is not recognized simply by a flowing beard or long hair. Yoga
is also not just a posture for physical fitness, stress relief, or for
earning fabulous wealth. ṭhese are by-products or external results of
performing asanas which is only one aspect of the astaṛga-yoga
system, the eight-fold yoga process explained below:

1. Yama - don'ts, or the regulations.
2. ṇiyama - dos, or the rules.
 3. Asana - physical postures for the body.

4. Pranayama - breathing exercises to puṛfy the mind.

5. Pratyahara - śutting the senses.

6. ḍharana - holding the Lord's divine form in the mind.

7. ḍhyana - intense meditation on the Lord's beautiful
transcendental form.

8. Samadhi - total absorption in our eternal spiṛtual love of

God.

chunks.source: Bhagavad-gita As It Is - His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada_565
chunks.author: Srila Prabhupada
chunks.text:
as steady as a lamp in a windless place. ṭEXṭS 20–23 yatroparamate cittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayā yatra caivātmanātmānaṁ paśyann ātmani tuṣyati

sukham ātyantikaṁ yat tad
buddhi-grāhyam atīndṛyam
vetti yatra na caivāyaṁ
sthitaś calati tattvataḥ

yaṁ labdhvā cāparaṁ lābhaṁ
manyate nādhikaṁ tataḥ
yasmin sthito na duḥkhena
guruṇāpi vicālyate

taṁ vidyād duḥkha-saṁyoga-
viyogaṁ yoga-saṁjñitam

chunks.author: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
chunks.text:
22.10 Chapter Eight

Bhakti-yoga:

ṭhe Supreme Yoga System

ṭEXṭ 14

tam imam te pravaksyami

yam avocam puranaghe

rsinam Srotu-kamanam

yogam sarvanga-naipunam

O most pious mother, I śall now explain unto you the ancient yoga system, which I explained formerly to the great sages. It is 
serviceable and practical in every way.
